results.possibleIdentity: Robert H. Cushman (also known as Robert Herman "Bob" Cushman, 1924–1996) was an electronics engineer and prominent technical journalist active during the 1960s and 1970s. He was employed at the Western Electric Engineering Research Center (ERC) in Princeton Junction, New Jersey, where he specialized in industrial electronics and advanced metal bonding techniques, such as thermal pulse bonding and fluxless metal joining. His technical contributions were published in Western Electric's journal 'The Engineer' and the 'Bell System Technical Journal.' Cushman held multiple patents assigned to Western Electric, including inventions related to capacitor termination. In addition to his engineering role, he had a long career as an editor for 'Electronic Design News' (EDN), where he was widely recognized for his influential coverage of the microprocessor industry during its infancy in the early 1970s. He was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a collaboration between artists and technical professionals, appearing in archival records alongside other Bell System engineers who promoted such multidisciplinary partnerships.
